channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill more than 20 as Polish prime minister warns against appeasement
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched heavy aerial attacks on Ukraine for a second night Saturday after the United States stopped sharing satellite images with the Ukrainian government, officials said. At least 22 people have been killed. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 12 people injured by 3 men shooting randomly at Toronto pub customers
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A dozen people were injured in a shooting at an eastern Toronto pub in what police called a reckless act of violence by three men who entered the bar and fired randomly without warning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis shows 'slight improvement' during fourth week in hospital, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis has apparently shown "slight improvement" and is responding will to oxygen therapy, the Vatican said on Saturday. This is the pope's fourth week at Rome's Gemelli Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea unveils its first nuclear-powered submarine
item.description: North Korea revealed its first nuclear-powered submarine, posing a threat to the United States and South Korea. Kim Jong Un has inspected its construction progress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian strike on Ukraine kills more than 10 after Trump threatens sanctions
item.description: Russia struck the Donetsk region in Ukraine on Friday, killing at least 11 and wounding 30 people according to Ukraine’s officials, hours after President Trump threatened to impose sanctions on Moscow in an effort to drive ceasefire negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: 2 days of clashes and revenge killings in Syria leave more than 1,000 people dead
item.description: A war monitoring group says the death toll from two days of clashes between Syrian security forces and loyalists of ousted President Bashar Assad and revenge killings that followed has risen to more than 1,000, including nearly 750 civilians, making it one of the deadliest outbreaks of violence sinc
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue And Sarah El Deeb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gaza plan criticized by US and Israel gets European support
item.description: Foreign ministers from France, Germany, Italy and the UK announced their support for Egypt's Gaza reconstruction plan, despite Israel and the US expressing reservations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bipartisan legislation seeks to rein in Erdogan's Turkey over ties to US foes
item.description: A bipartisan group of congressmen have taken issue with NATO ally Turkey aligning its foreign policy interests against the U.S. and its Western allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1 dead and several injured as tropical low tracks west across Australian east coast
item.description: BRISBANE, Australia (AP) — Flooding rains are set to continue lashing the Australian east coast even though it avoided the destructive winds of its first tropical cyclone in 51 years, officials said Saturday. One person was confirmed dead and several were injured.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis continues to rest in hospital as Vatican’s Holy Year proceeds without him
item.description: Pope Francis remained in Rome's Gemelli Hospital on Saturday recovering from double pneumonia as the Vatican’s Holy Year proceeded without him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From staff cuts to aid reductions, UN humanitarian agencies scramble in wake of US funding freeze
item.description: Trump administration freezes on U.S. foreign aid have led many United Nations organizations to cut staff, budgets and services in places as diverse as Afghanistan, Sudan, Ukraine and even the United States.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Shooting in Canada leaves a dozen wounded, police say
item.description: A shooting Friday evening at a pub in Toronto, Canada, left a dozen people wounded, as police continue to search for the suspect, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Polish government plans mandatory military training for adult men
item.description: The Polish government is working on a plan to prepare large-scale military training for all of its adult men, according to Prime Minister Donald Tusk.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 22 dead amid gang clashes in Ecuador's largest city
item.description: Gang infighting has left nearly two dozen people dead in Guayaquil, Ecuador's largest city. 14 people were arrested in the aftermath of the Thursday skirmish.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine's energy supplies as US cuts its access to satellite images
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia attacked Ukrainian energy facilities with dozens of missiles and drones during the night, officials said Friday, hobbling the country’s ability to deliver heat and light to its citizens and to power weapons factories vital to its defenses.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Polish leader Tusk plans large-scale military training for all adult males to boost reserves
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk said Friday that his government is working on a plan to prepare large-scale military training for every adult male in response to the changing security situation in Europe.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump calls it 'more difficult' to deal with Ukraine than Russia
item.description: President Trump on Friday warned that the U.S. would cut off its support for Ukraine if it did not agree to settle its war against Russia, telling reporters it has been “more difficult” to deal with Kyiv than with Moscow. "I think we're doing very well with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: 3 Bulgarians convicted of spying for Russia across Europe from base in UK
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Three Bulgarian nationals based in Britain were convicted Friday by a London jury of spying for Russia on what police said was “an industrial scale.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, WGN 9

item.title: Police in Bangladesh use batons and tear gas to disperse rally by banned Islamist group
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Police used batons and stun grenades on Friday to disperse thousands of members of the banned Hizbut Tahrir group as they marched near Bangladesh’s main Baitul Mokarram Mosque in the country’s capital.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aid efforts in Gaza slow as millions of promised USAID dollars do not arrive, agency officials say
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Trump administration’s cuts to USAID have frozen hundreds of millions of dollars in contractual payments to aid groups, leaving them paying out of pocket to preserve a fragile ceasefire, according to officials from the U.S. humanitarian agency.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Norway's crown princess condition worsens as she battles lung disease
item.description: Norwegian Crown Princess Mette-Marit's chronic lung disease is affecting "her ability to perform her duties," the Royal House of Norway announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN says 186 missing and at least 2 dead after migrant boats capsize off Yemen and Djibouti
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — At least two people have died and 186 others are missing after four boats carrying migrants from Africa capsized overnight in waters off Yemen and Djibouti, the U.N.'s migration agency said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans press Trump to resume military, intelligence aid to Ukraine
item.description: Republican lawmakers are starting to urge President Trump to reverse his decisions to pause U.S. military and intelligence assistance to Ukraine, warning that a prolonged stoppage of U.S. help for the war effort would have a seriously detrimental effect.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: EU leaders commit to working together after Trump signals that Europe must defend itself
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders on Thursday committed to working together to bolster the continent's defenses and to free up hundreds of billions of euros for security after U.S. President Donald Trump’s repeated warnings that he would cut them adrift to face the threat of Russia alone.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump envoy says Ukrainians 'brought it on themselves' after US pauses aid and intelligence sharing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ukraine was given “fair warning” by the White House before President Donald Trump this week ordered a pause on U.S. military assistance and intelligence sharing with Kyiv, a senior administration official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: One moment, calm waters. The next, a 900-pound dolphin landed on their boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 400 kilograms (900 pounds) appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada may halt electricity exports to US over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canadian Premier Doug Ford has threatened to cut off electricity exports to the US if President Trump's tariffs are enacted.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: With the Gaza ceasefire in limbo, Israel tries to impose an alternative plan on Hamas
item.description: Israel this week introduced what it said was a new U.S. ceasefire plan — different from the one it agreed to in January — and is trying to force Hamas to accept it by imposing a siege on the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered two new acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, in another setback to his battle to fight pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Arab countries are working on a postwar plan for Gaza
item.description: Arab foreign ministers met in Cairo on Monday for talks focusing on Egypt's plan to rebuild the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, hoping to counter President Donald Trump’s proposal to expel Palestinians from the coastal enclave and take it over.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A driver rams a car into crowd in Germany's Mannheim, leaving 2 dead and 11 injured
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver rammed a car into a crowd Monday in the southwestern German city of Mannheim, and authorities said two people were killed and 11 others injured, five of them severely.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two new episodes of acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel is criticized after it bars Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel faced sharp criticism as it stopped the entry of all food and other supplies into Gaza on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” for Hamas if a fragile ceasefire isn't extended.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Britain, France and Ukraine have agreed to work on a ceasefire plan to present to the United States, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday as European leaders assembled in London to discuss ending the war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
